question.text: What are the three types of participants in the currency derivatives market?

explanation.text: **The Logic:** The three types of participants are: (1) Hedgers — reduce risk from underlying exposure, (2) Speculators/Traders — take positions based on directional views, and (3) Arbitrageurs — exploit price differences across markets. **The Trap:** Brokers, dealers, importers etc. are categories of entities, not types of market participation. An importer could be a hedger; a bank could be any of the three. **Cross-Exam Context:** Market participant classification is tested in Series I, VIII, and XIII. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** The same entity may play different roles in different circumstances — a bank may hedge its own exposure while also speculating on another currency pair.
